channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Small aircraft crashes into Potomac River, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (DC News Now) -- DC Fire and EMS (DCFD) said a small aircraft crashed into the Potomac River Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump White House rescinds memo freezing federal money after widespread confusion
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's budget office on Wednesday rescinded a memo freezing spending on federal loans and grants, less than two days after it sparked widespread confusion and legal challenges across the country.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Are we all aliens? NASA's returned asteroid samples hold the ingredients of life from a watery world
item.description: Asteroid samples fetched by NASA hold not only the pristine building blocks for life but also the salty remains of an ancient water world..
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Australia wakes to more antisemitic graffiti after a discovery of explosives was revealed by police
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Three more incidents of antisemitic graffiti were found across Sydney on Thursday morning, leading Australian political leaders to warn of an escalation in hatred and decry as terrorism explosives found earlier in a trailer on the city’s outskirts.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Migrant TDA gang member breaks officer’s arm as 10 indicted in massive guns, drug running operation: police
item.description: Ten alleged members and associates of Tren de Aragua (TdA) gang have been indicted in a massive arms and drugs running operation spanning at least six states.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former US Sen. Bob Menendez gets 11 years in prison for taking bribes and acting as agent of Egypt
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez was sentenced Wednesday to 11 years in prison for accepting bribes of gold and cash and acting as an agent of Egypt — crimes his lawyer said he’s been mocked for as “Gold Bar Bob.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sheriff and deputies awarded $5M in 1st Amendment retaliation case
item.description: "Sheriff Reigenborn admitted that he looked at who donated to the other campaigns because that’s public information," attorney Iris Halpern said.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, NewsNation

item.title: Barbecue lover from Florida gets cozy welcome at Memphis restaurant
item.description: When the clock strikes 11 in Memphis, Cozy Corner is officially open and within seconds, booming with business.
item.dc:creator: Shay Simon, NewsNation

item.title: Flu map: These states are now at CDC's highest activity level
item.description: The map of flu activity across the United States continues to darken, both figuratively and literally, as case numbers spike upward in most states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KREX 5

item.title: Nonprofit teaches women construction, repair skills
item.description: The nonprofit helps women learn trades such as carpentry, plumbing, painting and electrical work. But safety is always first.
item.dc:creator: Donna Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Austin's police chief tells Hispanic leaders she isn't facilitating ICE
item.description: In the meantime, Hispanic organizations are uniting to debunk misinformation, and trying to educate people about their rights.
item.dc:creator: Jala Washington, NewsNation

item.title: Coast Guard stops boat carrying 21 illegal immigrants heading toward San Diego
item.description: The Coast Guard and CBP stopped a boat carrying 21 illegal immigrants from Mexico, Guatemala and El Salvador heading toward San Diego on Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: First independently developed jet breaks the sound barrier over the California desert
item.description: A sleek white aircraft became the first independently developed jet to break the sound barrier Tuesday, tearing through the air tens of thousands of feet above the Mojave Desert and a crowd of delighted onlookers.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: CVS gives some customers a way to get into locked cabinets
item.description: Instead of waiting for an employee to take pity on you and unlock the cabinets in the deodorant aisle, you may soon have another way to access the locked-up shampoo and ibuprofen you seek.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: North Carolina detention center inmate escapes via ventilation system: Sheriff
item.description: John Matthew Nigh's cellmates reportedly hid his escape by stuffing a mattress, making it look like he was sleeping in the cell.
item.dc:creator: Mekaela Muck, NewsNation

item.title: UNC Greensboro student builds community for former foster kids
item.description: "It was scary. Although I had moved a lot and I had been familiar with instability, I had never been with anyone I didn't know. I had never been with complete strangers," said Natalie Paul.
item.dc:creator: Madison Forsey, NewsNation

item.title: Broncos to supply helmets to every Colorado high school tackle football team
item.description: The Broncos have partnered with a football equipment company and the Colorado High School Activities Association to supply more than 15,000 brand-new Riddell Axiom helmets.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: These are the most romantic restaurants in the US, according to OpenTable
item.description: Whether you're looking for a spot with beautiful decor to set the mood or just somewhere fancy to impress that special someone, OpenTable has made a list just for you.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, NewsNation

item.title: Trump rescinds funding freeze but uncertainty lives on for wide array of environmental programs
item.description: A chaotic back-and-forth over President Donald Trump's freeze on federal grants and loans has farmers, activists, organizations and businesses wondering whether they will receive the funding they rely on.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 dogs get new homes after surviving plane crash that killed rescue flight pilot
item.description: Two dogs that survived an animal-rescue flight crash that killed the pilot have found new homes as the late aviator's friends work to honor him by helping more pets, a shelter director said Wednesday. The Nov.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Idaho House calls on Supreme Court to undo same-sex marriage ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court does not overturn its previous rulings absent a new case presenting a legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Cold-stunned green sea turtles are recovering at a Florida marine life center
item.description: JUNO BEACH, Fla. (AP) — An arctic blast that brought a rare snowfall to northern Florida last week left green sea turtles as far south as St. Augustine suffering from a condition known as cold stunning.
item.dc:creator: CODY JACKSON and FREIDA FRISARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alyssa Thomas heading to Mercury from Sun in trade, AP source says
item.description: The Phoenix Mercury were finalizing a deal to acquire Alyssa Thomas from the Connecticut Sun, a person familiar with the trade told The Associated Press on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Weeks after fire ravaged LA's Pacific Palisades, residents return to dig for mementos
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Scores of Los Angeles residents returned Tuesday to wildfire-ravaged Pacific Palisades after officials began letting people drive back into the scorched community, the first opportunity most have had to carefully scour the sites of their former homes.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US education agency investigating Denver schools over all-gender bathroom
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The U.S. Education Department said Tuesday it was investigating Denver Public Schools for alleged discrimination after the district converted a girl’s restroom on the second floor of a high school into an all-gender restroom while leaving another bathroom exclusive to boys.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Disturbing messages seen in empty lot in downtown Los Angeles are not nefarious: LAPD
item.description: A patch of undeveloped land in downtown Los Angeles is going viral on social media for some distressing messages seen from above, but police have confirmed that they found no illicit activity in the area.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The death toll from a New Year's Eve illegal fireworks explosion in Honolulu climbs to 5
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A fifth person has died from injuries suffered during a massive explosion of illegal fireworks at a Honolulu home over New Year's that prompted Hawaii authorities to reevaluate how they enforce the state's fireworks laws, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oklahoma proposal requires parents to provide immigration status when enrolling students
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Parents enrolling children in Oklahoma public schools will be required to provide proof of their child’s U.S. citizenship or legal immigration status under a proposed rule approved Tuesday by the State Board of Education.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Egg prices are soaring. Don't expect that to change anytime soon
item.description: Bird flu is forcing farmers to slaughter millions of chickens a month, pushing U.S. egg prices to more than double their cost in the summer of 2023. And it appears there may be no relief in sight, given the surge in demand as Easter approaches.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, MARK VANCLEAVE and DEE-ANN DURBIN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOJ fires prosecutors who worked on Trump criminal cases
item.description: Justice Department officials fired several prosecutors who worked on President Trump's criminal cases, saying they could not “trust” them.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by Trump shot, killed by sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, Fox 5

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
